summary.intent: Refactor the pr-watch Claude Code skill from a blocking sleep-loop polling architecture to an event-driven Monitor tool-based architecture that allows parallel work while watching a PR for review comments and CI failures.
summary.outcome: Successfully rewrote SKILL.md with Monitor-based background monitoring, created PR #89, monitored it through 4+ cycles, addressed 21+ review comments from copilot/devin bots, applied code fixes across multiple commits, and iteratively refined the skill based on review feedback.

repo: Plugin version management requires syncing both plugins/git/.claude-plugin/plugin.json and .claude-plugin/marketplace.json simultaneously
Conventional Commits scope-enum is restricted to: caffeine, catch-up, claude, dev, git, hookify
The repo uses a parallel autofix-pr remote session that can push fixes concurrently, causing push rejections and requiring rebase conflict resolution
CI failures (Lint Commit Messages, Validate Plugin) in this repo may be caused by GitHub billing issues rather than code problems
All PR reviewers (copilot-pull-request-reviewer, devin-ai-integration) are Bots — re-request review is not applicable

code.finding: Complete rewrite from blocking sleep-120 loop to Monitor tool pattern. Monitor script emits structured events (NEW_REVIEWS, CI_FAIL, PR_CONFLICT, TIMEOUT_IDLE, TIMEOUT_ABS) via stdout; Claude reacts to each event type in dedicated handlers (3a-3e).
Thread tracking uses thread_id:comment_count format (not just thread_id) to detect follow-up comments added to existing threads.
CI_FAIL Monitor event should emit only run_id (not run_id:name) because check names can contain colons, causing ambiguous parsing. Handler fetches name separately via gh api.

API error tracking uses a per-cycle API_FAIL flag aggregated into API_ERRORS counter at end of cycle, not per-call counting. PREV_CONFLICT state prevents duplicate conflict events on consecutive cycles.

workflow: Monitor tool's timeout_ms is ignored when persistent: true — script must self-terminate via exit with structured event output
GitHub has no official streaming/SSE/GraphQL Subscriptions for PR events; Activity Events API + ETag (304 Not Modified) is the most efficient polling approach
gh pr checks --watch is CI-only and exits on completion — unsuitable for persistent monitoring but useful as a one-shot post-push CI waiter
When remote autofix-pr session pushes concurrently: abort rebase, reset to remote HEAD, re-apply only un-merged local changes
Conflict markers inside bash heredocs in markdown (SKILL.md) appear as literal conflict markers — verify actual conflict location before resolving

friction: Concurrent autofix-pr remote session pushed fixes to the same file simultaneously, causing repeated push rejections and rebase conflicts requiring manual resolution
Context window exhausted mid-session requiring summary continuation, losing live state variables (UNFIXABLE_RUNS, cycle counts) that had to be reconstructed
sleep command was blocked by the shell environment, requiring ScheduleWakeup as a workaround for inter-cycle delays
Rebase conflicts inside markdown files with bash code blocks are visually confusing — conflict markers look like literal script content
CI failures were caused by GitHub billing issues (environment problem), not code issues — wasted investigation time before determining they were unfixable
open_items: CI failures for Lint Commit Messages and Validate Plugin remain unresolved due to GitHub billing/account issues — not a code problem but PR cannot pass CI
PR #89 is still in Draft state and has not been marked ready for review
Monitoring cycles 4+ were still in progress at transcript end — PR watch not yet concluded
The gh pr checks --watch integration was explicitly decided against but could be revisited for post-push CI acceleration
Plugin version bumped to git 2.16.0 / marketplace 3.11.0 but PR is not merged
